_comment: Catalog of stock Apple iPod firmware for `flashpod flash`. When no --firmware is given, flashpod offers this list, downloads the chosen image from base_url + file, caches it under ~/.cache/flashpod, and verifies it against sha256. The images are NOT bundled with flashpod (they are Apple's copyright); they are hosted as GitHub release assets. You can always bring your own with `--firmware <file>` (no network). Images ship as .bin.gz -- a gzipped raw Firmware-* image; load_firmware() dispatches on magic bytes, so a raw image or an Apple .ipsw works too if you supply one yourself. Apple ipsw naming was iPod_<family>.<version>.ipsw (family 1 = 1G/2G, 2 = 3G, 4/10 = 4G grayscale, 5 = photo, 11 = color); asset names keep that scheme and append the build date where it is known. build_date CANNOT be recovered from the file at runtime -- git and release downloads both reset mtimes, and the .ipsw zip timestamps are the 2006 repack date, not the build -- so it is recorded here. HARDWARE BOUNDARY: 2nd-generation (touch wheel) support first appeared in family-1 firmware 1.1.2. Anything older (0.0, 0.4) is 1G-ONLY -- the `generation` string is the only guidance the picker gives, so it must not claim 2G for those. We do not currently host 1.1.2 itself. LEGACY: the .ipsw assets remain on the release and are deliberately no longer referenced here; flashpod <= 0.1.7 ships a manifest that points at them, so deleting them would break those installs.
_models_comment: The iPod models flashpod can flash for, oldest first. `flashpod flash` asks which one you have and then offers only firmware whose `models` list includes it -- firmware is NOT interchangeable across models, and the 1G/2G split is the sharp one: 2G (touch wheel) support first appeared in family-1 firmware 1.1.2, so the 2001 images (0.0, 0.4) are 1G-only and would leave a 2G unusable.
_models_tell: 1G and 2G look identical -- same case, same screen, same four buttons ringing the wheel. The only difference you can check is by hand: the 1G's scroll wheel physically TURNS (it is mechanical), the 2G's does not move at all (touch-sensitive). Since that pair is also the one where a wrong pick matters (0.0 and 0.4 are 1G-only), the labels lead with that tell rather than with the model name.
_max_data_comment: No firmware here caps the data partition below the 128 GiB LBA28 ceiling (that ceiling binds every one of these images; see --lba48). HARDWARE-TESTED 2026-07-17: firmware 1.0 (0.0) boots a 128 GB card AND plays audio whose clusters land past 14 GB, and 1.0.4 (0.4) boots a 128 GB card -- neither has a low ceiling, so no max_data_gb field is set. (An earlier 0.0 broken-folder that looked like a size limit was an UNINITIALIZED card -- 0.0 does not auto-create iPod_Control, so a flashed-but-not-init'd data partition shows the broken folder regardless of size; run init.) A max_data_gb field (decimal GB) on an entry would clamp its data partition; none is needed today. `flashpod flash --max-data-gb N` can still cap a partition manually.
_salvage_comment: The 0.0/0.4 .bin.gz assets are ver=2 REPACKS. The original 2001 distribution files carried their directory at 0x4000 with payloads 0x800 BELOW their absolute devOffset (a logical/mis-packed layout); written verbatim the 1G boot ROM read garbage and broken-foldered. flashpod re-laid the osos/aupd payloads into the hardware-proven format-2 container (dir 0x4000+0x4200, payloads at absolute devOffset from 0x4400), which the 1G boots. build_date/version are unchanged -- same firmware, bootable packaging.

models.name: 3rd generation
models.tell: four buttons in a row above the wheel

models.name: 4th generation
models.tell: click wheel, buttons on the wheel, grayscale

firmwares.description: Last stock Apple-released firmware for 1G/2G iPods
Adds 20+ EQ presets, shuffle by album, track scrubbing, Contacts/Calendar/Clock, Korean and Chinese; iTunes 4.5 compatibility
Adds Italian, Dutch, Spanish, Brazilian Portuguese, Danish, Finnish, Norwegian and Swedish; 255-character filenames; fixes low-battery sleep wake and blue-and-white Power Macintosh G3 compatibility
Initial release for the original 1G iPod
Last stock Apple-released firmware for 4G grayscale. Apple shipped this as families 4 and 10 (two board revisions) but the firmware image is byte-identical, so one entry covers both
Last stock Apple-released firmware for iPod photo and the color-display revision. Apple shipped this as families 5 and 11 but the firmware image is byte-identical, so one entry covers both
